data.narrative:
Archer Materials is a public-stage quantum computing company currently valued at $60M, having raised $25M+. The peer universe comprises 5 same-sector, same-stage quantum computing companies: IonQ ($11.0B), Quantinuum ($10.0B), Rigetti Computing ($5.5B), D-Wave Quantum ($2.0B), and Infleqtion ($1.8B).

The peer-median method yields a $5.5B implied valuation based on the median of the five-company comparable set. This reflects the wide dispersion in the quantum computing public markets, where valuations range from $1.8B to $11.0B. Given limited methodological diversity in the available data, the triangulated range centers on the peer median: $4.7B (low), $5.5B (median), and $6.3B (high).

The analysis supports a valuation range of $4.7B to $6.3B, with a central estimate of $5.5B. This range is substantially higher than Archer's current $60M valuation and reflects comparability to the peer set on stage and sector. Key uncertainties include differentiation in technology maturity, path to commercialization, and cash burn rates relative to the comparable set—factors not captured in valuation multiples alone. The single-method reliance also limits confidence; additional approaches (DCF, revenue multiples, or patent-adjusted frameworks) would strengthen the analysis.
